rulings.Case Title: GOODMAN GROUP HOLDINGS LLC DBA ALLIANCE PROJECT VS. CUSTOM SPACES, INC. DBA CUSTOM SPACES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award And Obtain Judgment On Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER GOODMAN GROUP HOLDINGS LLC'S Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award And Obtain Judgment On Award. Petitioner Goodman Group Holdings LLC's motion to confirm arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. Goodman is entitled to judgment in its favor and against respondent Custom Spaces, Inc. in the amount of $13,765.99, comprised of $10,957 for the award, $521.81 in post award interest pursuant to the award, and $2,287.18 in fees for the petition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers and conform them to the tentative ruling. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP VS. YUNJI WILLA QIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Issued To Hall, Yee & Associates And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,810
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF QIAN & NEMECEK LLP's Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Issued To Hall, Yee & Associates And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,810 Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED in full. Hall Yee to comply with the subpoena within ten (10) days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINE MOIA VS. WILLIAMS-SONOMA INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Williams-Sonoma, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial Of Punitive Damages; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA INC.'S Motion To Bifurcate Trial Of Punitive Damages. Defendant Williams-Sonoma, Inc.'s motion to bifurcate trial of punitive damages is granted. No opposition filed. Per Code of Civil Procedure 3295(d), liability for punitive damages will be determined in the first phase of the trial and the amount of punitive damages will be determined in the second phase of the trial and no evidence of defendant's financial condition will be admitted as to punitive damages in the first phase of the trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Williams-Sonoma is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIMAR CORNEJO ET AL VS. KEY POINT CREDIT UNION A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARIMAR CORNEJO'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Marimar Cornejo's demurrer to all of the affirmative defenses in the answer to the second amended complaint filed by defendant American National Property and Casualty Company is overruled in its entirety. The demurrer was purportedly filed by Jamil Bey as the "authorized representative" of Ms. Cornejo. However, Mr. Bey, who is not an active member of the California Bar, may not represent Ms. Cornejo absent a court order such as appointment of a guardian ad litem and no such order has been issued. Consideration of the merits of the demurrer would be tantamount to allowing unauthorized practice of law. Moreover, it appears that Mr. Bey may be seeking to relitgate prior orders of this court that he is not a proper plaintiff. Mr. Bey's remedy is to seek relief from those orders from the Court of Appeal, not unilaterally re-assert himself into this case as Ms. Cornejo's "representative." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for American National is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BE & JDS, INC. VS. ANTHONY OBIDIAGHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT ANTHONY OBIDIAGHA'S Motion For Relief From Default. Defendant Anthony Obidiagha's motion to vacate the default entered against him on February 28, 2018 is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on the merits and there is no discernable legal prejudice to plaintiff BE & JDS, Inc. by the grant of the motion. Mr. Obidiagha has shown that he is entitled to have the default set aside per the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Moreover, the default was taken on the original complaint filed even though plaintiff had filed an amended complaint on January 23, 2018, which superseded the original, and plaintiff was required to serve the amended complaint in the same manner as the summons before entry of default because defendant had yet to appear in the case. The objections to the Emeziem and Obidiagha declarations are overruled. While the declarations refer to "information and belief," they also are based on personal knowledge. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Obidiagha is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA CAMPING VS. CHIPOLTLE SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT CHIPOTLE SERVICES, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings. Hearing required. Plaintiff Rebecca Camping is required to attend the hearing. If she is unable to do so, her counsel must explain why she is unable to do so and to provide dates when she will be able to appear in court. At the hearing Judge Kahn will ask a few questions of Ms. Camping regarding the matters at issue on this motion and will permit brief cross-examination by counsel for defendant Chipotle Services LLC. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUNE L. PAOLI VS. MICHAEL WHITE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT DAVID RUFFIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant David Ruffin, Jr.'s demurrer to the second and fourth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff June Paoli is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. The contract between Ms. Paoli and Mr. Ruffin is not reasonably susceptible to Ms. Paoli's proffered interpretation of that contract. The only contract language relied on by Ms. Paoli is Note 13 in sheet A-1 that "All labor and materials shall be guaranteed for a period of one (1) year from the date of completion of the work." That language refers to obligations of the contractor, not the architect. Absent clear and unambiguous language that the architect agreed to guarantee the work of the contractor, with whom Ms. Paoli entered into a separate contract, Ms. Paoli's proffered interpretation of the quoted language is untenable as a matter of law. Since Ms. Paoli has not provided any reason to believe that she can correct the deficiencies in her claims against Mr. Ruffin, leave to amend is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Ruffin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
